column1: What type of database is Redis defined as in terms of memory usage?
column2: An in-memory remote database.

column1: How many primary data structure types does Redis support?
column2: Five.

column1: Beyond performance, what two features allow Redis to scale from a prototype to a large-scale system?
column2: Replication and client-side sharding.

column1: What is the primary difference between Redis and a relational database regarding data relationships?
column2: Redis has no database-defined or -enforced way of relating data between structures.

column1: Which high-performance, key-value cache server is Redis most commonly compared to?
column2: Memcached.

column1: What are the two primary ways Redis handles data persistence to disk?
column2: Point-in-time dumps and append-only files.

column1: In Redis scaling, what is the specific purpose of master/slave replication?
column2: To support higher rates of read performance and handle server failover.

column1: What is 'sharding' in the context of Redis performance?
column2: A method of partitioning data into different pieces across multiple machines.

column1: Why is a SET used to track users who have voted on a specific article?
column2: To ensure that each user can only vote for the same article once.

column1: What does the HGETALL command return to the user?
column2: The entire hash (all keys and their associated values).

column1: Which command retrieves an item at a specific position within a Redis LIST?
column2: LINDEX

column1: Which Redis structure is best suited for storing article details like title and link?
column2: HASH

column1: What is the primary benefit of using append-only file (AOF) persistence configured to sync every second?
column2: It balances performance with high data durability by only risking one second of data.

column1: What is the default sorting order for Redis ZSET commands like ZRANGE?
column2: Ascending (lowest score to highest score).

column1: What is the purpose of the ZINCRBY command?
column2: To increment the score of a specific member in a ZSET.

column1: How many seconds are in one day, as used in the Redis scoring calculation?
Why is client-side sharding used in Redis deployments?
column2: To scale write performance by partitioning data across multiple Redis machines.

column1: What unique data structure feature allows Redis to be more efficient than memcached for managing lists of items?
column2: Native support for LIST and SET structures that allow direct item removal.

column1: What is the unique property of a Redis ZSET (Sorted Set)?
column2: Items are ordered by associated floating-point scores.

column1: Why is 'Unix time' (seconds since Jan 1, 1970) used for scoring in time-based applications?
column2: It is easily fetched in most programming languages and platforms.

column1: In the article voting example, how many points are added to an article's score per vote?
Which command is used to check if a specific string is a member of a SET?
column2: SISMEMBER

column1: Concept: Append-only file (AOF)
column2: A persistence method that logs every command that alters data to disk as it occurs.

column1: What does the DEL command return if it successfully deletes a value?
column2: The number of items that were deleted.

column1: Which command allows you to check if a key exists in Redis?
column2: EXISTS

column1: How does ZRANGE order the items it fetches from a ZSET?
column2: By their positions in ascending sorted order of their scores.

column1: Which command adds a member with a specific score to a ZSET?
column2: ZADD

column1: What are the index arguments for LRANGE to fetch every item in a LIST?
column2: 0 for the start index and -1 for the last index.

column1: What is the function of the Redis INCR command?
column2: To increment a counter and return the new value (often used for generating IDs).

column1: Which command fetches a single value from a specific field in a HASH?
column2: HGET

column1: When using SADD, what value does Redis return if the item already exists in the SET?
Which SET command removes a specific item from the set by its value?
column2: SREM

column1: Under what condition would ZREVRANGEBYSCORE be used instead of ZRANGE?
column2: When you need to fetch items in descending order based on their scores.

column1: Which LIST command is used to add a value to the right end of the list?
column2: RPUSH

column1: Why would a developer use ZINTERSTORE and EXPIRE together when filtering groups?
column2: To cache the result of an expensive intersection for a short period.

column1: What is the defining characteristic of a Redis SET compared to a LIST?
column2: A SET uses a hash table to ensure all strings are unique and is unordered.

column1: Why are random writes to Redis data consistently fast?
column2: The data is always stored in memory.

column1: Process: Creating a new article ID in the voting system example.
column2: The system calls INCR on a specific counter key (e.g., 'article:').

column1: What specific types of values can be stored within a Redis STRING?
column2: Strings, integers, or floating-point values.

column1: How is a Redis LIST structure implemented internally?
column2: As a linked list of strings.

column1: Which command removes a specific key-value pair from a Redis HASH?
column2: HDEL

column1: What is the standard character used by Redis developers to separate namespaces in key names?
column2: The colon character ($:$).

column1: What does a Redis HASH store?
column2: An unordered mapping of keys to values.

column1: What action does the LPOP command perform on a LIST?
column2: It removes and returns the value from the left end of the list.

column1: In the context of ZINTERSTORE, what does the 'aggregate=max' parameter ensure?
column2: The resulting score for a member is the maximum of the scores from the input sets.

column1: What mathematical operation do SINTER, SUNION, and SDIFF perform on SETs?
column2: Intersection, union, and difference, respectively.

column1: In the provided example, what is the formula for the 'cutoff' time for article voting?
column2: $time.time() - 7 \times 86400$

column1: When using ZINTERSTORE, what is the default score assigned to items coming from a SET?
Why would a developer choose Redis over a standard relational database for real-time analytics?
column2: To avoid the performance bottleneck of random reads/writes required to update on-disk rows.

column1: Concept: Master/Slave Replication
column2: A feature where slaves connect to a master, receive a full database copy, and then receive real-time write updates to scale read performance.

column1: In Redis, what does ZINTERSTORE do when combining a SET and a ZSET?
column2: It finds members present in both and combines their scores (treating SET members as having a score of 1).

column1: In the article voting logic, what happens to the 'voted' SET after one week?
column2: It is automatically deleted by Redis using the EXPIRE command.

column1: Which Redis data structure allows for pushing and popping items from both ends?
column2: LIST

column1: What command allows you to fetch article IDs from a ZSET in descending order of score?
column2: ZREVRANGE

column1: List the five data structures available in Redis.
column2: STRINGs, LISTs, SETs, HASHes, and ZSETs (Sorted Sets).

column1: In ZSETs, members must be unique, but can scores be duplicated?
column2: Yes.

column1: How do slave servers in a Redis setup keep their data current with the master?
column2: The master sends all writes to connected slaves to update their datasets in real time.

column1: Which command is used to make Redis automatically delete a key after a set amount of time?
column2: EXPIRE

column1: What is the typical use case for a Redis HASH in representing application objects?
column2: It is similar to a document in a document store or a row in a relational database.

column1: What occurs when the ZREM command is called on a ZSET member?
column2: The item is removed from the ZSET.

column1: Which HASH command stores a value at a specific sub-key?
column2: HSET

column1: Which command increments a numeric value stored inside a HASH field?
column2: HINCRBY

column1: How does Redis achieve high performance despite supporting complex data structures?
column2: By being an in-memory database and avoiding typical query parser/optimizer overhead.

column1: What distinguishes Redis HASHes from standard Redis STRINGs?
column2: HASHes allow you to group multiple related key-value pairs under a single top-level key.

column1: In a Sorted Set, what is the term for the unique string keys stored in the set?
column2: Members.

column1: How does Redis handle a GET request for a key that does not exist?
column2: It returns nil (which translates to None in Python).

column1: Which command fetches members from a ZSET based on a provided range of scores?
column2: ZRANGEBYSCORE

column1: What is the return value of SET if the operation is successful?
column2: OK

column1: Which command is used to fetch the data stored at a specific key in Redis?
column2: GET

column1: What is the command to return every item currently stored in a SET?
column2: SMEMBERS
